verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The Jews then murmured at Him, because He said, I am the Bread which came down from heaven; and they said, Is not this Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How is it then that He saith, I came down from heaven?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Whose god is their belly, and whose glory is in their shame” ( Philip. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 19 ), said Paul of certain persons, writing to the Philippians.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now that the Jews were of this character is clear, both from what has gone before, and from what they came and said to Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when He gave them bread, and filled their bellies, they said that He was a Prophet, and sought to make Him a King: but when He taught them concerning spiritual food, concerning eternal life, when He led them away from objects of sense, and spake to them of a resurrection, and raised their thoughts to higher matters, when most they ought to have admired, they murmur and start away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And yet, if He was that Prophet as they before asserted, declaring that he it was of whom Moses had said, “A Prophet shall the Lord your God raise up unto you of your brethren like unto me, unto Him shall ye hearken” ( Deut. xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 15 ); they ought to have hearkened to Him when He said, “I came down from heaven”; yet they hearkened not, but murmured.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They still reverenced Him, because the miracle of the loaves was recent, and therefore they did not openly gainsay Him, but by murmuring expressed their displeasure, that He did not give them the meal which they desired.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And murmuring they said, “Is not this the son of Joseph?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whence it is plain, that as yet they knew not of His strange and marvelous Generation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And so they still say that He is the son of Joseph, and are not rebuked; and He saith not to them, “I am not the Son of Joseph”; not because He was his son, but because they were not as yet able to hear of that marvelous Birth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if they could not bear to hear in plain terms of His birth according to the flesh, much less could they hear of that ineffable Birth which is from above.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If He revealed not that which was lower to them, much less would He commit to them the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Although this greatly offended them, that He was born from a mean and common father, still He revealed not to them the truth, lest in removing one cause of offense He should create another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then said He when they murmured?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “No man can come unto Me, except the Father which hath sent Me draw Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Manichæans spring upon these words, saying, “that nothing lies in our own power”; yet the expression showeth that we are masters of our will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For if a man cometh to Him,” saith some one, “what need is there of drawing?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the words do not take away our free will, but show that we greatly need assistance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He implieth not an unwilling comer, but one enjoying much succor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then He showeth also the manner in which He draweth; for that men may not, again, form any material idea of God, He addeth,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Not that any man hath seen God, save He which is of God, He hath seen the Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How then,” saith some one, “doth the Father draw?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This the Prophet explained of old, when he proclaimed beforehand, and said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “They shall all be taught of God.” ( Isa. liv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou the dignity of faith, and that not of men nor by man, but by God Himself they shall learn this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And to make this assertion credible, He referred them to their prophets.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If then ‘all shall be taught of God,’ how is it that some shall not believe?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because the words are spoken of the greater number.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, the prophecy meaneth not absolutely all, but all that have the will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the teacher sitteth ready to impart what he hath to all, and pouring forth his instruction unto all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And I will raise him up in the last day.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not slight here is the authority of the Son, if so be that the Father leadeth, He raiseth up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He distinguisheth not His working from that of the Father, (how could that be?) but showeth equality of power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As, therefore, after saying in that other place, “The Father which hath sent Me beareth witness of Me,” He then, that they might not be over-curious about the utterance, referred them to the Scriptures; so here, that they may not entertain similar suspicions, He referreth them to the Prophets, whom He continually and everywhere quoteth, to show that He is not opposed to the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But what of those,” saith some one, “who were before His time?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Were not they taught of God? why then the special application of the words here?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because of old they learned the things of God by the hands of men, but now by the Only-begotten Son of God, and by the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then He addeth, “Not that any man hath seen the Father, save He which is of God,” using this expression here not with reference to the cause, but to the manner of being.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since had He spoken in the former sense, we are all “of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And where then would be the special and distinct nature of the Son?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But wherefore,” saith some one, “did He not put this more clearly?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because of their weakness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if when He said, “I am come down from heaven,” they were so offended, what would they have felt had He added this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He calleth Himself, ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 48 ,) “the bread of life,” because He maintaineth our life both which is and which is to be, and saith, “Whosoever shall eat of this bread shall live for ever.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By “bread” He meaneth here either His saving doctrines and the faith which is in Him, or His own Body; for both nerve the soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet in another place He said, “If a man hear My saying, he shall never taste of death.” ( c. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And they were offended; here they had no such feeling perhaps, because they yet respected Him on account of the loaves which had been made.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe how He distinguisheth between His bread and the manna, by causing them to hear the result of each kind of food.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For to show that the manna afforded them no unusual advantage, He added,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Your fathers did eat manna in the wilderness, and are dead.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He then establisheth a thing most likely to persuade them, that they were deemed worthy of greater things than their fathers, (meaning those marvelous men who lived in the time of Moses,) and so, after saying that they were dead who ate the manna, He addeth,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that eateth of this bread, shall live for ever.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nor hath He put “in the wilderness” without a cause, but to point out that the supply of manna was not extended to a long time, nor entered with them into the land of promise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But this “bread” was not of the same kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And the bread that I will give is My flesh, which I will give for the life of the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here one might reasonably enquire, how this was a fit season for these words, which neither edified nor profited, but rather did mischief to those who had been edified; for “from that time,” saith the Evangelist, “many of His disciples went back,” saying, “This is a hard saying; who can hear it?” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 60 ); since these things might have been entrusted to the disciples only, as Matthew hath told us that He discoursed with them apart. ( Mark iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 34: see Matt. xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then shall we say?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is the profit of the words?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Great is the profit and necessity of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because they pressed upon Him, asking for bodily food, reminding Him of the food provided in the days of their forefathers, and speaking of the manna as a great thing, to show them that all those things were but type and shadow, but that the very reality of the matter was now present with them, He mentioneth spiritual food.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But,” saith some one, “he ought to have said, Your fathers did eat manna in the wilderness, but I have given you bread.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the interval between the two miracles was great, and the latter of them would have appeared inferior to the former, because the manna came down from heaven, but this, the miracle of the loaves, was wrought on earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore they sought food “coming down from heaven,” He continually told them, “I came down from heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if any one enquire why He introduced the discourse on the Mysteries, we will reply, that this was a very fitting time for such discourses; for indistinctness in what is said always rouses the hearer, and renders him more attentive.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They ought not then to have been offended, but rather to have asked and enquired.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But now they went back.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If they believed Him to be a Prophet, they ought to have believed His words, so that the offense was caused by their own folly, not by any difficulty in the words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe how by little and little He led them up to Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here He saith that Himself giveth, not the Father; “The bread that I will give is My flesh, which I will give for the life of the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But,” saith some one, “this doctrine was strange to them and unusual.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And yet John at an earlier period alluded to it by calling Him “Lamb.” ( c. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But for all that, they knew it not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I know they did not; nay, neither did the disciples understand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if as yet they had no clear knowledge of the Resurrection, and so knew not what, “Destroy this Temple, and in three days I will raise it up” ( John ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 19 ), might mean, much more would they be ignorant of what is said here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For these words were less clear than those.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since that prophets had raised men from the dead, they knew, even if the Scriptures have not spoken so clearly on the subject, but not one of them ever asserted that any man had eaten flesh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Still they obeyed, and followed Him, and confessed that He had the words of eternal life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this is a disciple’s part, not to be over-curious about the assertions of his teacher, but to hear and obey him, and to wait the proper time for the solution of any difficulties.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How then,” saith some one, “was it that the contrary came to pass, and that these men ‘went back’?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was by reason of their folly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when questioning concerning the “how” comes in, there comes in with it unbelief.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So Nicodemus was perplexed, saying, “How can a man enter into his mother’s womb?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So also these are confounded, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How can this man give us his flesh to eat?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If thou seekest to know the “how,” why askedst not thou this in the matter of the loaves, how He extended five to so great a number?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because they then only thought of being satisfied, not of seeing the miracle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But,” saith some one, “their experience then taught them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then by reason of that experience these words ought to have been readily received.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For to this end He wrought beforehand that strange miracle, that taught by it they might no longer disbelieve what should be said by Him afterwards.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Those men then at that time reaped no fruit from what was said, but we have enjoyed the benefit in the very realities.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore it is necessary to understand the marvel of the Mysteries, what it is, why it was given, and what is the profit of the action.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We become one Body, and “members of His flesh and of His bones.” ( Eph. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let the initiated follow what I say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In order then that we may become this not by love only, but in very deed, let us be blended into that flesh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is effected by the food which He hath freely given us, desiring to show the love which He hath for us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account He hath mixed up Himself with us; He hath kneaded up His body with ours, that we might be a certain One Thing, like a body joined to a head.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this belongs to them who love strongly; this, for instance, Job implied, speaking of his servants, by whom he was beloved so exceedingly, that they desired to cleave unto his flesh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they said, to show the strong love which they felt, “Who would give us to be satisfied with his flesh?” ( Job xxxi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore this also Christ hath done, to lead us to a closer friendship, and to show His love for us; He hath given to those who desire Him not only to see Him, but even to touch, and eat Him, and fix their teeth in His flesh, and to embrace Him, and satisfy all their love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then return from that table like lions breathing fire, having become terrible to the devil; thinking on our Head, and on the love which He hath shown for us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Parents often entrust their offspring to others to feed; “but I,” saith He, “do not so, I feed you with Mine own flesh, desiring that you all be nobly born, and holding forth to you good hopes for the future.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He who giveth out Himself to you here, much more will do so hereafter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I have willed to become your Brother, for your sake I shared in flesh and blood, and in turn I give out to you the flesh and the blood by which I became your kinsman.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This blood causeth the image of our King to be fresh within us, produceth beauty unspeakable, permitteth not the nobleness of our souls to waste away, watering it continually, and nourishing it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The blood derived from our food becomes not at once blood, but something else; while this doth not so, but straightway watereth our souls, and worketh in them some mighty power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This blood, if rightly taken, driveth away devils, and keepeth them afar off from us, while it calleth to us Angels and the Lord of Angels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For wherever they see the Lord’s blood, devils flee, and Angels run together.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This blood poured forth washed clean all the world; many wise sayings did the blessed Paul utter concerning it in the Epistle to the Hebrews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This blood cleansed the secret place, and the Holy of Holies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if the type of it had such great power in the temple of the Hebrews, and in the midst of Egypt, when smeared on the door-posts, much more the reality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This blood sanctified the golden altar; without it the high priest dared not enter into the secret place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This blood consecrated priests, this in types cleansed sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if it had such power in the types, if death so shuddered at the shadow, tell me how would it not have dreaded the very reality?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This blood is the salvation of our souls, by this the soul is washed, by this is beautiful, by this is inflamed, this causeth our understanding to be more bright than fire, and our soul more beaming than gold; this blood was poured forth, and made heaven accessible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Awful in truth are the Mysteries of the Church, awful in truth is the Altar.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A fountain went up out of Paradise sending forth material rivers, from this table springeth up a fountain which sendeth forth rivers spiritual.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By the side of this fountain are planted not fruitless willows, but trees reaching even to heaven, bearing fruit ever timely and undecaying.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If any be scorched with heat, let him come to the side of this fountain and cool his burning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it quencheth drought, and comforteth all things that are burnt up, not by the sun, but by the fiery darts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it hath its beginning from above, and its source is there, whence also its water floweth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Many are the streams of that fountain which the Comforter sendeth forth, and the Son is the Mediator, not holding mattock to clear the way, but opening our minds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This fountain is a fountain of light, spouting forth rays of truth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By it stand the Powers on high looking upon the beauty of its streams, because they more clearly perceive the power of the Things set forth, and the flashings unapproachable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as when gold is being molten if one should (were it possible) dip in it his hand or his tongue, he would immediately render them golden; thus, but in much greater degree, doth what here is set forth work upon the soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Fiercer than fire the river boileth up, yet burneth not, but only baptizeth that on which it layeth hold.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This blood was ever typified of old in the altars and sacrifices of righteous men, This is the price of the world, by This Christ purchased to Himself the Church, by This He hath adorned Her all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as a man buying servants giveth gold for them, and again when he desireth to deck them out doth this also with gold; so Christ hath purchased us with His blood, and adorned us with His blood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They who share this blood stand with Angels and Archangels and the Powers that are above, clothed in Christ’s own kingly robe, and having the armor of the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nay, I have not as yet said any great thing: they are clothed with the King Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now as this is a great and wonderful thing, so if thou approach it with pureness, thou approachest for salvation; but if with an evil conscience, for punishment and vengeance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For,” It saith, “he that eateth and drinketh unworthily” of the Lord, “eateth and drinketh judgment to himself” ( 1 Cor. xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 29 ); since if they who defile the kingly purple are punished equally with those who rend it, it is not unreasonable that they who receive the Body with unclean thoughts should suffer the same punishment as those who rent it with the nails.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe at least how fearful a punishment Paul declareth, when he saith, “He that despised Moses’ law dieth without mercy under two or three witnesses; of how much sorer punishment, suppose ye, shall he be thought worthy, who hath trodden under foot the Son of God, and hath counted the blood of the covenant, wherewith he was sanctified, an unholy thing?” ( Heb. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Take we then heed to ourselves, beloved, we who enjoy such blessings; and if we desire to utter any shameful word, or perceive ourselves hurried away by wrath or any like passion, let us consider of what things we have been deemed worthy, of how great a Spirit we have partaken, and this consideration shall be a sobering of our unreasonable passions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For how long shall we be nailed to present things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How long shall it be before we rouse ourselves?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How long shall we neglect our own salvation?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us bear in mind of what things Christ has deemed us worthy, let us give thanks, let us glorify Him, not by our faith alone, but also by our very works, that we may obtain the good things that are to come, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom and with whom, to the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, now and ever and world without end.
